aiashley — global digest Generated 08:09 UTC

Wednesday, July 22, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The US-Iran conflict dominated international headlines as American forces conducted their 11th consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets, with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth revealing the war has cost $37.5 billion to date [BBC, Al Jazeera, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. President Trump threatened to strike Iran's underground nuclear facility near Natanz, while Secretary of State Rubio stated the US remains open to negotiations but accused Tehran of not being serious [Al Jazeera, BBC]. In Ukraine, President Zelensky dismissed army commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi following days of street protests triggered by the earlier removal of popular Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, replacing him with General Mykhailo Drapatyi [BBC, Le Monde, RFI]. France became the first European country to pass legislation banning social media for children under 15, with President Macron hailing it as a major step forward in child protection [BBC, RFI]. US-Canada relations deteriorated further as Ottawa cancelled a joint ceremony for the Gordie Howe International Bridge after Trump announced 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, invoking the 1930 Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act [Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. Meanwhile, Germany and Azerbaijan signed strategic economic agreements as Berlin seeks to diversify energy supplies away from Russia [RFI].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

US Launches 11th Consecutive Night of Strikes on Iran as War Costs Reach $37.5 Billion

  • US Central Command conducted strikes targeting Iranian military operations centres, maritime capabilities, aircraft hangars, drone storage facilities, and military logistics infrastructure
  • Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified the war has cost $37.5 billion, up from $29 billion in mid-May, and requested $67 billion in supplemental Pentagon funding
  • Air defences were activated in Tehran, with explosions reported in Tabriz, Chabahar, Konarak, and Bushehr; Iran warned of reprisals after Trump threatened to strike the underground nuclear site near Natanz
  • Secretary of State Rubio stated the US is open to negotiations but accused Iran of not being serious about talks, warning that Iranian control of the Strait of Hormuz would set a dangerous precedent
Coverage
BBC WorldFocused on military operations and Trump's threat to nuclear facilities Al JazeeraEmphasized Rubio's comments on negotiations and included Iranian state media reports on air defence activation The HinduDetailed Hegseth's Senate testimony and Democratic criticism of war spending Globe and MailHighlighted congressional pushback and concerns about 'another forever war' The EconomistAnalysis piece comparing situation to Obama's foreign policy approach
02 Conflict

Zelensky Dismisses Army Chief Syrskyi After Days of Street Protests Over Military Leadership

  • Ukrainian President Zelensky fired Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi following the earlier dismissal of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, which triggered nationwide protests
  • Syrskyi, 60, was replaced by General Mykhailo Drapatyi, 43, a popular battle-hardened commander who thanked his predecessor for strengthening the Ukrainian army
  • Fedorov, 35, had been praised for modernizing the armed forces and fighting corruption, while Syrskyi was seen as a Soviet-school commander resistant to radical changes
  • Protests entered their fifth day with demonstrators in Kyiv demanding Syrskyi's removal and Fedorov's return, representing Zelensky's most urgent domestic political challenge since the full-scale invasion
Coverage
BBC WorldBalanced coverage of both commanders' records and Drapatyi's appointment Le MondeArticle headline available but content not accessible RFI EnglishEmphasized the political crisis and pressure on Zelensky, included protester testimonies about personal losses
03 Policy

France Becomes First European Country to Ban Social Media for Under-15s

  • French Parliament approved legislation requiring age verification for all social media users, banning those under 15 from accessing platforms from January 2027
  • The law will be implemented in two stages: new accounts blocked for under-15s from September 2026, and verification required for all existing accounts by January 2027
  • President Macron hailed the move as a major step forward, stating France is leading Europe in protecting children and teenagers
  • Critics raised concerns over privacy, the efficacy of age-verification tools, and the risk of young people bypassing restrictions
Coverage
BBC WorldDetailed implementation timeline and noted skepticism about viability RFI EnglishEmphasized France as first EU country to follow Australia's lead, included parliamentary vote count of 279-81
04 Economics

Canada Cancels Joint Bridge Ceremony with US After Trump Announces 50% Tariffs

  • Canada cancelled the planned joint US-Canada ceremony for the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor after Trump announced 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods
  • Trump invoked Section 338 of the 1930 Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act, accusing Canada of discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol, and dairy products
  • Canada will hold its own ceremony on July 24, with the bridge still expected to open to traffic on July 27
  • The tariff list targets hundreds of Canadian goods including extensive alcohol-related products, prompting observers to note its unusual focus on cocktail ingredients
Coverage
The HinduStraightforward reporting on ceremony cancellation and infrastructure minister's statement Globe and MailExtensive coverage including historical context of Smoot-Hawley Act and detailed analysis of tariff list contents
05 Geopolitics

Germany and Azerbaijan Sign Strategic Economic Agreements to Deepen Energy and Trade Ties

  • German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev signed a Joint Declaration on Strategic Agenda and established an Azerbaijan-Germany Business Council
  • Azerbaijan began supplying gas to Germany this year as the EU moves away from Russian energy imports
  • Merz described the South Caucasus as a key connectivity hub linking Europe and Asia, referencing the Middle Corridor trade route that bypasses Russia
  • The talks also addressed Azerbaijan's reconciliation process with Armenia, with Merz congratulating Aliyev on progress toward a peace agreement
Coverage
RFI EnglishComprehensive coverage of economic agreements and regional geopolitical context including Armenia relations
Also Today
  • Ukrainian drones strike Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second time in four days, injuring 15 workers BBC World
  • Health worker evacuated from DRC being monitored for Ebola at London hospital as precautionary measure The Guardian
  • Indian opposition leader Rahul Gandhi released after detention during student-led protests over education system Al Jazeera
  • Trump administration approves nuclear deal potentially allowing Saudi Arabia uranium enrichment capability Globe and Mail
  • Protests erupt in Bologna after Moroccan-born man dies while being restrained by police, injuring 64 officers BBC Europe
  • New Zealand issues Level 3 travel advisory urging citizens to avoid Middle East due to US-Iran conflict RNZ
  • France and Germany developing joint roadmap to address EU's growing trade deficit with China by September RFI English
  • At least 53 people dead in ferry disaster in Guyana Le Monde
  • Indonesia's free meal program chief resigns citing health after budget cuts and corruption scandals Nikkei Asia
  • UN report finds one in three people globally cannot afford a healthy diet despite decline in hunger rates The Guardian
